questions.question: According to the lesson, what does the 'Next Right Choice' rule mean?

options.text: You must always make the perfect food choice or start over from scratch
One slip-up means your progress is ruined and you need a new plan
One off-day does not undo your progress; just refocus on the next meal with self-compassion
You should plan every meal a week in advance to avoid mistakes

questions.explanation: The 'Next Right Choice' rule means that the goal is progress, not perfection. You cannot fail. One off-day does not undo your progress. Be compassionate with yourself and simply refocus on the next meal.

questions.question: Which of the following is listed as a budget-friendly brain food?

options.text: Organic salmon fillets
Imported specialty cheese
Beans, lentils, oats, and frozen vegetables
Fresh sushi and acai bowls

questions.explanation: Beans, lentils, oats, frozen vegetables, bananas, and eggs are highlighted as among the most affordable AND brain-healthy foods available. Frozen produce is just as nutritious as fresh and lasts much longer.

questions.question: What strategy does the lesson recommend when you feel overwhelmed by all the nutritional information?

options.text: Try to implement all changes at once to get it over with
Stop the course and come back in a few months
Focus on just ONE change for the week, master it, and layer on the next when ready
Ask someone else to manage your diet for you

questions.explanation: When feeling overwhelmed, the lesson advises simplifying by picking just ONE change for the week. Master that one component, build confidence, and then layer on the next change when you feel ready.

questions.question: What is the overall approach this course takes toward nutritional changes?

options.text: Strict dieting with calorie counting and food elimination
Nourishing yourself with small, sustainable changes -- not restriction or punishment
Following a specific meal plan with no flexibility
Avoiding all processed foods completely and immediately

questions.explanation: The course emphasizes that this journey is about nourishing yourself -- not restricting or punishing yourself. Every small step counts, and building new habits takes practice, patience, and self-compassion.

questions.question: Why does the lesson say that initial discomfort when changing eating habits can actually be a good sign?

options.text: It means the changes are not working and you should try something different
It indicates a food allergy that needs medical attention
It is often a sign that the changes are taking hold as your body and mind adjust to new patterns
It means you are eating too much fiber and should reduce intake

questions.explanation: The lesson explains that the first few weeks are often the most challenging because your body and mind are adjusting to new patterns. The initial discomfort is often a sign that the changes are taking hold.

title: Common Roadblocks and How to Overcome Them - Quiz
